# Contract Review Checklist: Versioned Upstream CLI Contract and SDK Upgrade Workflow

**Purpose**: Validate requirements quality for upstream CLI contract, provenance, security, coverage, and promotion decisions after task generation
**Created**: 2026-07-17
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It validates the requirements themselves, not implementation behavior.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all supported, observed, and candidate baseline state transitions fully documented, including which transitions are prohibited without promotion? [Completeness, Spec FR-013, Spec FR-030]
- [ ] CHK002 Are command contract fields complete enough to cover arguments, flags, inherited options, aliases, hidden/deprecated state, execution mode, output metadata, and provenance without relying on command-name matching? [Completeness, Spec FR-014]
- [ ] CHK003 Are coverage levels defined with enough required fields for typed, raw, process-oriented, unsupported, legacy, and incomplete decisions? [Completeness, Spec FR-019, Spec FR-020]
- [ ] CHK004 Are maintainer-approved SDK contract requirements complete enough to identify operation IDs, bindings, input/output contracts, source references, coverage level, and test references? [Completeness, Spec FR-020, Plan Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK005 Are generated facts, manifest suggestions, upgrade bundles, source evidence, and approved SDK contract responsibilities explicitly separated across the requirements? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-027, Spec FR-030]
- [ ] CHK006 Are JSON Schema generation, schema-version rejection, migration, and external validation requirements fully represented in the specification, not only in the plan/tasks? [Gap, Plan T014, Contracts baseline-artifacts]
- [ ] CHK007 Are runtime compatibility policy requirements complete enough to cover tested ranges, newer untested versions, override behavior, and documentation generation? [Completeness, Spec FR-025, Spec FR-033, Spec SC-018]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK008 Is "explicit maintainer decision" defined with clear evidence, actor, artifact, and persistence requirements? [Clarity, Spec FR-016, Spec FR-028, Spec FR-030]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "sufficient binary/source provenance" broken down into exact required fields, including commit, tag validation, binary digest, platform, generator identity, and collection method? [Clarity, Spec FR-017, Spec SC-004]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "isolated environment without repository secrets or user configuration" clarified with concrete boundaries for environment variables, config directories, network access, timeouts, and output limits? [Clarity, Spec FR-023]
- [ ] CHK011 Is "degraded mode" for help-parser fallback quantified with required trust-level metadata, review items, and promotion-blocking rules? [Clarity, Spec FR-032, Spec SC-017]
- [ ] CHK012 Are "potentially breaking", "breaking", "additive", "documentation-only", and "provenance-only" severity labels defined with unambiguous classification rules? [Clarity, Spec FR-015, Spec SC-015]
- [ ] CHK013 Is "current upstream command" defined clearly enough to handle hidden commands, deprecated commands, aliases, legacy compatibility paths, and disappearing command paths? [Clarity, Spec FR-007, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK014 Are "raw" and "process-oriented" support differentiated clearly enough that reports and docs cannot imply typed SDK support? [Clarity, Spec FR-019, Spec FR-031, Spec SC-016]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK015 Are the P1 story priorities consistent with the implementation strategy that treats US1 as MVP and defers collection/observer behavior? [Consistency, Spec User Stories, Tasks Implementation Strategy]
- [ ] CHK016 Are offline-gate requirements consistent across spec, plan, and tasks, especially where tasks mention candidate diff status in the coverage gate? [Consistency, Spec FR-011, Spec FR-022, Tasks T065]
- [ ] CHK017 Are source evidence, approved SDK contract, and generator boundaries consistent between AGENTS guidance, plan structure, and task paths? [Consistency, Plan Project Structure, Tasks T003, Tasks T004, Tasks T017]
- [ ] CHK018 Are observer requirements consistent about being non-blocking, non-promoting, idempotent, and separate from ordinary PR/push CI? [Consistency, Spec FR-022, Spec SC-010, Tasks T075-T083]
- [ ] CHK019 Are upgrade suggestion requirements consistent about suggestions remaining incomplete after generation and after apply-manifest-suggestions? [Consistency, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-024, Tasks T069, Tasks T073]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK020 Can the under-30-second maintainer outcome be objectively measured from a documented command/report shape without depending on unstated UI expectations? [Measurability, Spec SC-001]
- [ ] CHK021 Are 100% coverage criteria scoped to a defined fixture/input population so they are objectively measurable and not open-ended? [Measurability, Spec SC-002, Spec SC-003, Spec SC-005]
- [ ] CHK022 Are byte-identical canonical contract criteria precise about excluded volatile metadata and where detached observation metadata is stored? [Measurability, Spec SC-008, Spec FR-018]
- [ ] CHK023 Are idempotency criteria precise enough to distinguish no working-tree diff, no duplicate tracking issue, no duplicate PR, and reuse/update behavior? [Measurability, Spec SC-013, Spec US5 Acceptance]
- [ ] CHK024 Are failure outcomes tied to documented exit-code categories for invalid artifacts, collection failures, source/binary mismatches, unresolved breaking changes, and invalid usage? [Measurability, Spec FR-021]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK025 Are exception scenarios documented for missing executable, timed-out traversal, partial inspection, oversized output, checksum mismatch, and source/binary disagreement? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Spec SC-009, Spec SC-014]
- [ ] CHK026 Are promotion scenarios documented for clean candidates, provenance-only candidates, additive candidates, potentially breaking candidates, and breaking candidates? [Coverage, Spec FR-030, Spec SC-015]
- [ ] CHK027 Are fallback collection scenarios covered for upstream exporter, release contract asset, Go helper/exporter, and help-parser fallback? [Coverage, Spec FR-032, Tasks T040-T049]
- [ ] CHK028 Are compatibility scenarios covered for newer untested CLI versions, supported versions, override path, and repeated warning suppression? [Coverage, Spec FR-033, Spec SC-018]
- [ ] CHK029 Are recovery scenarios defined for failed upgrade preparation, superseded candidates, and rerunning observer/preparation workflows? [Gap, Spec US4, Spec US5, Tasks T076, Tasks T080]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK030 Are requirements defined for presence-sensitive values across omitted, null/None, empty string, zero, and false, including when an unset sentinel is required? [Edge Case, Spec FR-028, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK031 Are requirements defined for dynamic enums, deprecated enum values, aliases, strict/open enum policy, and operation-specific enum scope? [Edge Case, Spec FR-028]
- [ ] CHK032 Are imperative validation rules specified with normalized constraint categories such as requires, conflicts_with, exactly_one, at_least_one, and required_together? [Edge Case, Spec FR-028]
- [ ] CHK033 Are output contract edge cases specified for optional field additions, removed fields, type changes, non-structured output, and curated fixture confidence? [Edge Case, Spec FR-014, Spec FR-020, Spec SC-012]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK034 Are performance requirements scoped separately for offline check, local collection, canonicalization, and observer workflows? [Clarity, Plan Performance Goals]
- [ ] CHK035 Are security requirements complete enough to cover checksum trust source, archive extraction safety, secret redaction, user configuration isolation, and CI action pinning? [Completeness, Spec FR-023, Constitution V, Tasks T078]
- [ ] CHK036 Are offline testability requirements explicit for all blocking gates, including no live account, no server, no network, and fixture/provenance traceability? [Completeness, Spec FR-011, Constitution IV]
- [ ] CHK037 Are runtime dependency constraints reflected in the feature requirements when new collector/reporting/schema functionality is planned? [Consistency, Plan Constraints, Constitution SDK Constraints]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK038 Are assumptions about the upstream `multica-ai/multica` release artifacts, checksums, source availability, and exporter availability documented with fallback behavior? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions, Spec FR-032]
- [ ] CHK039 Are dependencies on existing manifests, generated CLI artifacts, fake executable fixtures, and source-link audit behavior identified with migration requirements? [Dependency, Tasks T037-T039, Tasks T089]
- [ ] CHK040 Are requirements explicit about which artifacts are checked in, which are temporary outputs, and which are generated review bundles? [Clarity, Spec FR-017, Spec FR-024, Plan Project Structure]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK041 Is there any conflict between "collect from selected executable" and the security requirement to avoid user configuration and repository secrets? [Conflict, Spec FR-003, Spec FR-023]
- [ ] CHK042 Is there any ambiguity about whether candidate diff checks belong in the US1 offline MVP or only after US2/US3 candidate artifacts exist? [Ambiguity, Spec US1 Acceptance 2, Tasks T065]
- [ ] CHK043 Is the owner of approval decisions clear when both human maintainer and agent-assisted review are mentioned across AGENTS guidance and feature artifacts? [Ambiguity, Spec FR-028, AGENTS Review Rules]
- [ ] CHK044 Are tasks and requirements aligned on the location of contract artifacts, especially `contracts/sdk-contract.yaml` versus checked-in generated artifacts under `src/multica_py/_generated/`? [Ambiguity, Plan Storage, Tasks T007, Tasks T038]
